prevalence.trend: Increasing due to urbanization and pollution

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on GINA guidelines: recurrent wheezing, breathlessness, chest tightness, cough; confirmed by spirometry showing reversible airflow obstruction (FEV1/FVC <0.7 with >12% improvement post-bronchodilator)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted GINA with focus on spirometry availability; peak flow meters in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Inhaled corticosteroids (ICS) + long-acting beta-agonists (LABA); short-acting beta-agonists for relief; education on trigger avoidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High air pollution in cities like Delhi; limited access to inhalers in rural areas; misdiagnosis as respiratory infections
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness via campaigns; affordable generic inhalers; integration with Ayushman Bharat for coverage
